title: Empirical Pit-Window Hazard Features
summary: Pit stopping is a hazard/event-timing problem: probability spikes at common stint ages and race-progress windows rather than varying smoothly with tyre life alone.
rationale: Pit stopping is a hazard/event-timing problem: probability spikes at common stint ages and race-progress windows rather than varying smoothly with tyre life alone. Public race-strategy models frame pit epochs as the central decision variables.
implementation_hint: For each Race/Year/Compound/Stint/PitStop bucket, build smoothed out-of-fold target/hazard rates by TyreLife bin, LapNumber bin, and RaceProgress bin. Add features like bucket historical stop rate next lap, cumulative non-pit survival, distance to modal pit lap, and distance to modal tyre life.
expected_effect: Should add high-signal priors beyond generic degradation interactions, especially around the narrow laps where positive labels cluster.
risk: Target encoding must be out-of-fold/group-aware; naive full-data rates can leak target distribution and overfit rare race-compound buckets.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
